op.template: A {place} produces {a} {items} in the morning and {b} more in the afternoon.

merged_prompt:
A depot produces 55 crates in the morning and 71 more in the afternoon. A wholesaler then orders 2 times the running count, and that order becomes the new running count. An overnight delivery then raises the running count by 42. A supplier then adds 39 more to the running count. Inspectors visit the depot 13 times per season. The depot employs 28 workers year-round. The next cycle turns out 3 times the running count, which replaces it. The next cycle turns out 3 times the running count, which replaces it. Workers then split the running count into 9 equal lots; only a single lot moves on as the running count. Later, 68 additional crates join the running count. A supplier then adds 57 more to the running count. The site was founded 29 years ago. Inspectors visit the depot 80 times per season. Workers then split the running count into 2 equal lots; only a single lot moves on as the running count. Later, 21 additional crates join the running count. The depot employs 25 workers year-round. The running count is then divided evenly among 5 trucks; keep only one truck's share as the new running count. Shipping damage then costs the running count 39. Shipping damage then costs the running count 7. What is the final running count?

Answer with a single integer.
